title: Mock Test 19 — Full Syllabus High Difficulty

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: PROFLIGATE : THRIFT :: GARRULOUS : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'trait : the quality it conspicuously lacks'.

**Working.** A profligate (wasteful) person lacks thrift; a garrulous (excessively talkative) person lacks reticence (reserve in speech).

**Answer.** reticence.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A number increased by $20\%$ becomes $90$. The original number is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Increasing by $20\%$ multiplies the number by $1.2$.

**Working.**
$$x\times 1.2 = 90 \Rightarrow x = \frac{90}{1.2} = 75$$

**Answer.** $75$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Six people sit equally spaced around a circular table. If A sits directly opposite D, the number of seats between A and D (counting the shorter way) is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Directly opposite means half the table away.

**Working.** With $6$ seats, opposite seats have $\tfrac{6}{2}-1=2$ seats between them on each side.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $3, 7, 15, 31, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $31\times 2 + 1 = 63$.

**Answer.** $63$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many squares (of all sizes) are there in the $3\times3$ grid shown?

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 grid of unit squares.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum of squares of sizes $1,2,3$: $3^2+2^2+1^2$.

**Working.** $9+4+1=14$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 2?

figure.caption: A cube net: a horizontal row of faces 1, 2, 3, 4 with face 5 above face 2 and face 6 below face 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='20' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='110' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='36' y='95'>1</text><text x='76' y='95'>2</text><text x='116' y='95'>3</text><text x='156' y='95'>4</text><text x='76' y='55'>5</text><text x='76' y='135'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a straight row of four faces, faces two apart are opposite.

**Working.** With 2 as the front, 1 is left, 3 is right and 4 is back; front (2) is opposite back (4).

**Answer.** 4.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'New safety measures were introduced to ____ the risk of roof collapse.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Safety measures aim to reduce risk.

**Working.** 'Mitigate' means to make less severe; 'aggravate' would worsen the risk.

**Answer.** mitigate.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express the right side as a power of the same base.

**Working.** $32=2^5$, so $x=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A invests $12000$ for $6$ months and B invests $9000$ for $8$ months. Their profit-sharing ratio A : B is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share is proportional to (capital $\times$ time).

**Working.** A $=12000\times 6=72000$; B $=9000\times 8=72000$; ratio $=1:1$.

**Answer.** $1:1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded once in half and a single hole is punched through both layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded once in half with one hole punched through the folded sheet.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='70' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='90' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each fold layer carries one hole.

**Working.** One fold gives $2$ layers; one punch makes $1\times 2 = 2$ holes.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Safety fuse burns at 120 s per metre. The burning time for a 1.5 m length, in seconds, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Time $=$ rate $\times$ length.

**Working.**
$$t=120\times1.5=180\ \text{s}$$

**Answer.** 180 s (3 minutes).

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A machine has a mean time between failures (MTBF) of 90 h and a mean time to repair (MTTR) of 10 h, as shown. Compute its steady-state availability.

figure.caption: Uptime vs downtime
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='35' width='180' height='20' fill='#86efac' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='200' y='35' width='30' height='20' fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#475569'/><text x='70' y='28' font-size='9'>MTBF = 90 h</text><text x='188' y='72' font-size='9'>MTTR=10 h</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Availability is the fraction of time the machine is operable.

**Working.**
$$A = \frac{MTBF}{MTBF + MTTR} = \frac{90}{90 + 10} = 0.9$$

**Answer.** $A = \mathbf{0.9}$ (90%).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Newton's second law of motion states that
options: force equals the rate of change of momentum
every action has an equal and opposite reaction
a body stays at rest unless acted upon by a force
energy can neither be created nor destroyed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The second law links force to momentum change.

**Working.** $F=\dfrac{d(mv)}{dt}=ma$ for constant mass.

**Answer.** force equals the rate of change of momentum.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using Simpson's $\tfrac{1}{3}$ rule with $2$ sub-intervals ($h=1$), the estimate of $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2}x^{2}\,dx$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Simpson's 1/3 rule fits a parabola through three equally spaced points to approximate the area.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 150 Q120 20 200 60' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.12)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='160' x2='40' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='120' y1='160' x2='120' y2='75' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='200' y1='160' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='40' cy='150' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><circle cx='120' cy='75' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><circle cx='200' cy='60' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='34' y='174' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>x0</text><text x='114' y='174' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>x1</text><text x='194' y='174' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>x2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $S=\dfrac{h}{3}\big[f_0+4f_1+f_2\big]$.

**Working.** $f(0)=0,\,f(1)=1,\,f(2)=4$.
$$S=\frac{1}{3}\big[0+4(1)+4\big]=\frac{8}{3}\approx 2.67$$
Simpson's rule is exact for quadratics, matching the true value.

**Answer.** $\tfrac{8}{3}\approx 2.67$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Depreciation in cost accounting is best described as
options: the systematic allocation of an asset's cost over its useful life
an actual cash outflow each year
the resale value of the asset
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Depreciation is a non-cash expense.

**Working.** It spreads the capital cost across the years of use.

**Answer.** the systematic allocation of an asset's cost over its useful life.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a projectile launched at $20\,\text{m/s}$ at $30^\circ$ (take $g=9.81\,\text{m/s}^2$), the maximum height reached is _____ m. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum height $=\dfrac{u^2\sin^2\theta}{2g}$.

**Working.**
$$H=\frac{20^2(\sin 30^\circ)^2}{2(9.81)}=\frac{100}{19.62}=5.10.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 5.10\,\text{m}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock with a higher friction angle, all else equal, will have
options: a steeper failure envelope and higher shear strength at a given normal stress
no dependence of strength on normal stress
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Envelope slope is $\tan\phi$.

**Working.** Larger $\phi$ steepens the envelope, raising $\tau$ at any $\sigma_n$.

**Answer.** a steeper failure envelope and higher shear strength at a given normal stress.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the bord-and-pillar plan shown, square pillars are 20 m wide and galleries are 5 m wide (centre-to-centre spacing 25 m). The percentage extraction (first working) is _____ % (one decimal).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='0' y='0' width='240' height='175' fill='#1f2937'/><g fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'><rect x='20' y='20' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='170' y='20' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='20' y='95' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='95' y='95' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='170' y='95' width='50' height='50'/></g><text x='10' y='192' font-size='11'>pillar 20 m, gallery 5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Bord-and-pillar layout (plan)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Extraction ratio $e=1-\dfrac{W^2}{(W+B)^2}$ for square pillars (W = pillar side, B = gallery width).

**Working.**
$$e=1-\frac{20^2}{25^2}=1-\frac{400}{625}=1-0.64=0.36=36\%$$

**Answer.** $36\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Monthly Compounding
questions.stem: $\textsf{Rs}\,10{,}000$ is invested at a nominal $12\%$ per year compounded monthly. Its value after $1$ year is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: An external spoil dump covers a base area of 50,000 m² to an average height of 20 m. Its loose storage capacity, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $\approx$ base area $\times$ average height.

**Working.**
$$V=50{,}000\times20=1{,}000{,}000\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 1,000,000 m³.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Growth Time
questions.stem: The number of years for a sum to triple at $8\%$ compounded annually is _____ . Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a parallel (split) ventilation circuit, the pressure drop across each branch is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Branches share common end nodes.

**Working.** The pressure difference across them is identical.

**Answer.** the same for every branch.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The stability of a large external spoil dump is improved by
options: increasing its overall slope angle as much as possible
benching/terracing the dump and limiting its overall slope angle
placing the wettest material at the toe
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dump stability follows the same slope principles as pit walls.

**Reasoning.** Terracing breaks the height into stable lifts, flattening the overall slope toward the angle of repose; good foundation preparation and drainage prevent pore-pressure-driven failure.

**Answer.** Terrace the dump and limit overall slope angle.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The dominant air-pollution problem from opencast mining is
options: fugitive dust (particulate matter) from drilling, blasting, loading and haulage, controlled by water spraying and green belts
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mechanical operations generate airborne dust.

**Reasoning.** Drilling, blasting, material handling and truck haulage raise particulate matter; control measures include water spraying of roads, dust extraction, and perimeter plantation/green belts.

**Answer.** Fugitive particulate dust.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A square pillar has width $w=8$ m and height $h=4$ m (see figure). Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S_p=S_1\left(0.778+0.222\,\dfrac{w}{h}\right)$ with $S_1=10$ MPa, the pillar strength, in MPa, is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='55' y='40' width='90' height='90' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='55' y1='150' x2='145' y2='150' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='1'/><text x='100' y='166' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' fill='#2563eb'>w = 8 m</text><line x1='165' y1='40' x2='165' y2='130' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='1'/><text x='170' y='90' font-size='11' fill='#dc2626'>h = 4 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Pillar of width w and height h

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: To compare alternatives with different service lives, the most convenient method is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Annualising removes the effect of differing horizons.

**Working.** EUAC (or a common multiple of lives) gives a fair comparison.

**Answer.** the equivalent uniform annual cost.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A uniaxial stress of $120\,\text{MPa}$ acts. The normal stress on a plane whose normal makes $30^\circ$ with the stress axis is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_n=\sigma\cos^2\theta$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_n=120\cos^2 30^\circ=120(0.75)=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: At an effective output of 240 m³/h over a 12-h operating shift, the bank volume produced, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shift output $=$ effective rate $\times$ operating hours.

**Working.**
$$V=240\times12=2880\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 2880 m³.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The Geological Strength Index (GSI) is primarily used as input to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** GSI scales the Hoek-Brown constants $m_b$, $s$, $a$.

**Working.** It links field observations to rock-mass strength.

**Answer.** the Hoek-Brown rock-mass strength criterion.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The intact Hoek-Brown criterion is $\sigma_1=\sigma_3+\sigma_c\sqrt{m\,\sigma_3/\sigma_c+s}$. For $\sigma_c=100\,\text{MPa}$, $m=10$, $s=1$ and $\sigma_3=0$, $\sigma_1$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Set $\sigma_3=0$, $s=1$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_1=0+100\sqrt{0+1}=100.$$

**Answer.** $100\,\text{MPa}$ (the UCS).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a projectile launched at $20\,\text{m/s}$ at $30^\circ$ (take $g=9.81\,\text{m/s}^2$), the horizontal range is _____ m. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Range $=\dfrac{u^2\sin 2\theta}{g}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\frac{20^2\sin 60^\circ}{9.81}=\frac{346.41}{9.81}=35.31.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 35.31\,\text{m}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Periodic Rate
questions.stem: A nominal annual rate of $18\%$ is compounded monthly. The interest rate per month is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Periodic rate $=r/m$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{18}{12}=1.5\%.$$

**Answer.** $1.5\%$ per month.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Compute the vertical in-situ stress at a depth of $400\ \text{m}$ in rock of density $2500\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$ (use $g=9.81\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$, figure) in MPa (two decimals).

figure.caption: Vertical stress with depth
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='120' height='100' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='100' cy='110' r='5' fill='#1f2937'/><line x1='100' y1='25' x2='100' y2='45' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#a)'/><defs><marker id='a' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 0 L3 6 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='34' y='140' font-size='8'>rho=2500, H=400 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_v=\rho g H$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_v=2500\times9.81\times400=9.81\times10^6\ \text{Pa}=9.81\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $9.81$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Compared with CPM, the PERT technique is
options: probabilistic, using three time estimates per activity
concerned only with cost, not time
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PERT handles uncertainty.

**Working.** It uses optimistic/most-likely/pessimistic estimates; CPM uses a single deterministic time.

**Answer.** probabilistic, using three time estimates.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Stone-dust or water-trough explosion barriers installed in roadways are designed to
options: increase the airflow to the face
be dislodged by the pressure wave ahead of an explosion flame, releasing inert dust/water that quenches and arrests the flame
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Barriers suppress a propagating explosion.

**Reasoning.** The pressure pulse preceding the flame tips the loaded shelves/troughs, dispersing stone dust or water into the path of the advancing flame so it is cooled and extinguished before it can spread further.

**Answer.** Release inert dust/water to quench the flame.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which elementary row operations leave the determinant of a square matrix UNCHANGED?
options: Adding a multiple of one row to another
Multiplying a row by a non-zero scalar $k$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Effect of elementary operations on the determinant.

**Working.**
- Add a multiple of one row to another — determinant unchanged (TRUE).
- Swap two rows — sign flips (FALSE).
- Multiply a row by $k$ — determinant scales by $k$ (FALSE).
- Transpose — $\det(A^{T})=\det(A)$ (TRUE).

**Answer.** Options (a) and (d).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The minimum air velocity to be maintained at a working face is governed mainly by
options: statutory limits and the need to dilute gas, dust and heat
the depth of the shaft only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Face velocity must remove contaminants and heat.

**Working.** Statutory minima and dilution requirements set it.

**Answer.** statutory limits and dilution of gas/dust/heat.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Under uniaxial stress of $100\,\text{MPa}$, the maximum shear stress in the material is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For uniaxial stress, $\tau_{max}=\dfrac{\sigma}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\frac{100}{2}=50.$$

**Answer.** $50\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A reduced (quadrantal) bearing is expressed
options: as an angle between 0° and 90° measured from north or south, with the quadrant indicated (NE, SE, SW, NW)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quadrantal bearings reference the nearer meridian.

**Reasoning.** The angle is measured from north or south, toward east or west, lying between 0° and 90°, e.g. N30°E or S45°W; the surveyor's compass reads these.

**Answer.** 0°–90° from N/S with quadrant.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A closed traverse has total error in latitude $\sum L = 0.3$ m and total error in departure $\sum D = 0.4$ m. Determine the closing (linear misclosure) error in metres.

figure.caption: Closed traverse with misclosure
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,150 90,40 170,70 150,150' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='30' cy='150' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='170' cy='70' r='3'/><circle cx='150' cy='150' r='3'/></g><line x1='150' y1='150' x2='30' y2='150' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='20' y='172' font-size='8'>sumL=0.3 m, sumD=0.4 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The linear closing error of a traverse is the vector sum of the latitude and departure misclosures.

**Working.**
$$e=\sqrt{(\sum L)^2+(\sum D)^2}=\sqrt{0.3^2+0.4^2}=\sqrt{0.25}=0.5\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $0.5$ m.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The vertical stress at a depth of $200\,\text{m}$ in rock of unit weight $25\,\text{kN/m}^3$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_v=\gamma H$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_v=25\times 200=5000\,\text{kPa}=5\,\text{MPa}.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y'+y=e^{2x}$, the coefficient $A$ in the particular solution $y_p=A e^{2x}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Method of undetermined coefficients: substitute $y_p=Ae^{2x}$.

**Working.**
$$2Ae^{2x}+Ae^{2x}=e^{2x}\Rightarrow 3A=1\Rightarrow A=\tfrac13\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using the tributary-area method for the layout shown (vertical stress 10 MPa, pillar 20 m, gallery 5 m), compute the average pillar stress (MPa).

figure.caption: Tributary area
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='80' height='80' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='64' height='64' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='150' y='105' font-size='9' fill='#475569'>sigma_v=10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tributary area: $\sigma_p = \sigma_v \left(\dfrac{W+B}{W}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p = 10 \left(\frac{25}{20}\right)^2 = 10 \times 1.5625 = 15.625$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{15.625}$ MPa.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Using an overall slope angle of 47.7°, the horizontal offset from crest to toe for a 60 m deep pit, in m, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Offset $=\dfrac{\text{depth}}{\tan(\text{overall slope})}$.

**Working.**
$$x=\frac{60}{\tan 47.7^{\circ}}=\frac{60}{1.098}=54.6\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 54.6 m.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Smooth-wall (cushion/trim) blasting differs from presplitting in that the perimeter holes are
options: fired first, before any production holes
fired last in the round, with light decoupled charges, to trim the wall after the bulk is removed
charged more heavily than production holes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Smooth blasting trims the wall at the end.

**Reasoning.** The lightly charged, decoupled perimeter holes are detonated last (after the buffer/production rows), shaving a smooth final face; presplitting instead fires the perimeter first.

**Answer.** Fired last with light decoupled charges.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The ultimate pit limit of an opencast mine defines
options: the final pit outline that maximises total profit, its boundary set where SR equals BESR
the depth of the first bench only
the angle of repose of the dump
the width of the haul road
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The pit is expanded only while each increment is profitable.

**Reasoning.** The ultimate (final) pit is the largest outline for which every boundary increment still satisfies actual SR $\le$ BESR; expanding further would add waste worth more than the ore it exposes.

**Answer.** Profit-maximising final outline (boundary at SR = BESR).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A total of $60\,\text{m}^3$/s splits naturally between parallel airways of resistance $1$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$. The quantity in the $R=1$ airway is _____ m$^3$/s. Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: Air splits between a low-resistance (R=1) and a high-resistance (R=3) parallel route.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q\propto\dfrac{1}{\sqrt R}$. Shares are $\dfrac{1/\sqrt R_i}{\sum 1/\sqrt R_i}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt1}=1,\ \frac{1}{\sqrt3}=0.5774,\ \text{sum}=1.5774.$$
$$Q_1=60\times\frac{1}{1.5774}=38.04.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 38.04\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A dry planar rock slope fails along a discontinuity dipping at $\beta=30^\circ$ with friction angle $\phi=35^\circ$ and negligible cohesion. Compute the factor of safety.

figure.caption: Plane failure on a slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,130 180,130 180,40' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='60' y1='130' x2='180' y2='55' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><text x='90' y='120' font-size='8'>beta=30</text><text x='120' y='80' font-size='8'>phi=35</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a dry frictional plane, $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\beta}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{\tan35^\circ}{\tan30^\circ}=\frac{0.7002}{0.5774}=1.21$$

**Answer.** $1.21$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops a total pressure of $500\ \text{Pa}$ while passing $40\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. Compute the air power (kW).

figure.caption: Fan air power
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='70' cy='65' r='30' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='70' y1='65' x2='70' y2='40'/><line x1='70' y1='65' x2='92' y2='78'/><line x1='70' y1='65' x2='48' y2='78'/></g><line x1='100' y1='65' x2='200' y2='65' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#fp)'/><defs><marker id='fp' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='115' font-size='8'>H=500 Pa, Q=40 m^3/s</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=H\times Q$.

**Working.**
$$P=500\times40=20000\ \text{W}=20\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $20$ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: At the EOQ ($Q=500$, $H=\textsf{Rs}\,4$/unit-year), the annual holding cost is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Holding cost $=\dfrac{Q}{2}\,H$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{500}{2}\times4=250\times4=1000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,1000$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: The transit rule for traverse adjustment distributes the closing error in proportion to the
options: magnitude of the latitude (or departure) of each line
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Transit weights by component magnitude.

**Reasoning.** Correction to a latitude $=$ total latitude error $\times\dfrac{|\text{latitude of line}|}{\sum|\text{latitudes}|}$ (similarly for departures); it is preferred when angular measurements are more precise than linear ones.

**Answer.** Magnitude of latitude/departure.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A lamp of weight $50\,\text{N}$ hangs from the midpoint of a wire; each half of the wire makes $30^\circ$ with the horizontal. The tension in the wire is _____ N.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Vertical equilibrium: $2T\sin\theta=W$.

**Working.**
$$T=\frac{50}{2\sin 30^\circ}=\frac{50}{1}=50.$$

**Answer.** $50\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: With vertical stress $\sigma_v=12.5\,\text{MPa}$ and extraction ratio $e=0.6$, the tributary-area pillar stress is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=\frac{12.5}{0.4}=31.25.$$

**Answer.** $31.25\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For the longwall face shown (face length 200 m, web depth 0.8 m, seam height 2 m, coal density 1.4 t/m³), compute the coal produced per shear (tonnes).

figure.caption: Longwall face
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='100' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='14' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='40' font-size='8' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>face L=200 m</text><rect x='30' y='44' width='12' height='86' fill='#94a3b8'/><text x='60' y='85' font-size='8'>web 0.8 m, h=2 m</text><text x='60' y='105' font-size='8'>rho=1.4 t/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage per shear = face length × web × height × density.

**Working.**
$$T = 200 \times 0.8 \times 2 \times 1.4 = 448$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{448}$ t per shear.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A $2\,\text{kg}$ block, initially at rest on a frictionless floor, is pushed by a constant $12\,\text{N}$ force over $5\,\text{m}$. Its final speed is _____ m/s. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Work-energy theorem: $Fd=\tfrac12 mv^2$.

**Working.**
$$v=\sqrt{\frac{2Fd}{m}}=\sqrt{\frac{2(12)(5)}{2}}=\sqrt{60}=7.75.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 7.75\,\text{m/s}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In Barton's Q-system, the parameter $J_n$ represents
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $J_n$ = joint set number.

**Working.** It quantifies how many joint sets cut the rock mass.

**Answer.** the number of joint sets.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A fully grouted rock bolt (see figure) has diameter $d=25\ \mathrm{mm}$, anchored length $L=2\ \mathrm{m}$, and a grout–rock bond strength $\tau=1\ \mathrm{MPa}$. The pull-out capacity ($\pi d L \tau$), in kN, is _____ (nearest integer).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='140' height='150' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#78716c'/><line x1='90' y1='30' x2='90' y2='150' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='4'/><rect x='78' y='150' width='24' height='8' fill='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='158' x2='90' y2='185' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bo)'/><defs><marker id='bo' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='0' orient='auto'><path d='M0 6 L4 0 L8 6 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='96' y='90' font-size='9'>L=2 m, d=25 mm</text><text x='96' y='182' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>pull-out</text></svg>
figure.caption: Grouted rock bolt

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pull-out capacity $=\tau\times$ bond surface area $=\tau\,\pi d L$.

**Working.**
$$P=1\times10^{6}\times\pi\times0.025\times2=157080\ \text{N}\approx157\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** $157$ kN.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Belt conveyors are preferred over trucks for waste/ore haulage when the requirement is
options: continuous, high-volume transport up steep grades on a fixed route
selective transport of individual large boulders
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Conveyors are continuous, fixed-path systems.

**Reasoning.** They move large, steady tonnages economically, can climb grades far steeper than truck ramps, and have low energy/labour cost per tonne — provided the route is fixed.

**Answer.** Continuous high-volume haulage up steep grades on a fixed route.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Square pillars of width $10\,\text{m}$ are separated by $10\,\text{m}$ wide openings. With vertical stress $\sigma_v=10\,\text{MPa}$, the tributary-area pillar stress $\sigma_p=\sigma_v\left(\dfrac{W_p+W_o}{W_p}\right)^2$ is _____ MPa.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' width='200' height='200'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#f8fafc' stroke='#94a3b8'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='40' height='40' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='110' y='30' width='40' height='40' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='110' width='40' height='40' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='110' y='110' width='40' height='40' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='120' height='120' fill='none' stroke='#1d4ed8' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><text x='38' y='54' font-size='9' fill='#475569'>pillar</text><text x='95' y='185' font-size='9' fill='#1d4ed8'>tributary area</text></svg>
figure.caption: Plan of square pillars (grey) with the tributary area (dashed) of one pillar.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\sigma_v\left(\dfrac{W_p+W_o}{W_p}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=10\left(\frac{20}{10}\right)^2=10\times4=40.$$

**Answer.** $40\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: For the same network, determine the total float (in weeks) available on the non-critical chain A–C.

figure.caption: Project network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='30' cy='80' r='14' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='84' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>S</text><circle cx='130' cy='40' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='130' y='44' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>A=2</text><circle cx='130' cy='120' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='130' y='124' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>B=4</text><circle cx='230' cy='40' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='230' y='44' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>C=3</text><circle cx='230' cy='120' r='16' fill='#fef3c7' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='230' y='124' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>D=5</text><circle cx='285' cy='80' r='12' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='285' y='84' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>E</text><g stroke='#475569' fill='none'><path d='M44 74 L114 46'/><path d='M44 86 L114 114'/><path d='M146 40 L214 40'/><path d='M146 120 L214 120'/><path d='M246 46 L275 74'/><path d='M246 114 L275 86'/></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total float on a chain equals the critical-path length minus the length of that chain.

**Working.**
$$F = 9 - (2+3) = 9 - 5 = 4$$

**Answer.** The A–C chain has **4 weeks** of total float.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: On the ISRM strength scale, a rock with UCS of about $5\,\text{MPa}$ is classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ISRM strength bands by UCS.

**Working.** UCS around $5\,\text{MPa}$ falls in the weak range (roughly $5$–$25\,\text{MPa}$).

**Answer.** weak rock.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For RQD $=75$, $J_n=6$, $J_r=2$, $J_a=2$, $J_w=0.66$, SRF $=2.5$, Barton's $Q$ is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\dfrac{\text{RQD}}{J_n}\cdot\dfrac{J_r}{J_a}\cdot\dfrac{J_w}{\text{SRF}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{75}{6}\cdot\frac{2}{2}\cdot\frac{0.66}{2.5}=12.5\times1\times0.264=3.30.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 3.30$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For an M/M/1 queue with $\rho=0.6$, the average number of units in the system $L_s$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L_s=\dfrac{\rho}{1-\rho}$.

**Working.**
$$L_s=\frac{0.6}{0.4}=1.5.$$

**Answer.** $1.5$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A longwall block contains 5 Mt of coal in situ and the overall extraction (recovery) is 85%. The recoverable coal, in Mt, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recoverable $=$ in-situ $\times$ recovery.

**Working.**
$$R=5\times0.85=4.25\ \text{Mt}$$

**Answer.** 4.25 Mt.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project has expected duration $20$ days and standard deviation $2$ days. The probability of finishing within $22$ days corresponds to $z=1.0$; using $\Phi(1.0)=0.8413$, this probability is _____ . Round to four decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $z=\dfrac{T_s-T_e}{\sigma}$, then read $\Phi(z)$.

**Working.**
$$z=\frac{22-20}{2}=1.0,\qquad \Phi(1.0)=0.8413.$$

**Answer.** $0.8413$.
